title: Shattered Reflections in the Conservatory
openingIncident: At 8:45 pm, Eleanor Whitcombe, renowned art dealer, was found dead in a sunlit conservatory table set with glass, bottle, plate and knife. Three people had been present during the final minutes, and each gave a different account. Nothing outside the room is needed to identify the killer.

suspects.name: Gareth Pembroke
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, Clara Haversham helped me set the bottle down on the side table. We left the room together immediately afterwards. Victor Lyle and Eleanor Whitcombe were still inside.
suspects.alibiClaim: Clara Haversham and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

suspects.name: Clara Haversham
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, I helped Gareth Pembroke set the bottle down on the side table. We then left together and remained together until the body was found at 8:45 pm.
suspects.alibiClaim: Gareth Pembroke and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

suspects.name: Victor Lyle
suspects.statement: I left at 8:30 pm. I never touched the knife and did not go near the glass.
suspects.alibiClaim: I had already left the room by 8:30 pm and did not return.

clues.title: The Edge of Knife
clues.detail: A fresh line of blood runs along one edge of the knife, and its point has a new bend. Eleanor Whitcombe has a narrow wound consistent with that edge.

clues.title: Ring beneath Bottle
clues.detail: The fresh circular ring beneath the bottle is crossed by a narrow blood droplet from the attack whose edge remains unbroken. Gareth Pembroke and Clara Haversham both state that they set the bottle down on the side table at 8:35 pm before leaving together.

clues.title: Fibres on Knife
clues.detail: Teal-and-cream tweed fibres are caught around the handle of the knife. Victor Lyle's teal tweed jacket cuff has a newly frayed cuff edge, exposing the same unusual teal-and-cream check.

clues.title: Fragment at Glass
clues.detail: A curved black lacquer chip edged in gold is lodged inside the handle joint of the glass on top of a narrow blood droplet from the attack. The black-and-gold fountain-pen cap worn by Victor Lyle has a fresh curved gap in the lacquer; the exposed edges visibly fit the fragment.

clues.title: Crack in Plate
clues.detail: A crack runs across the plate. A continuous yellowed glue line is visible deep inside the break.

solution.motive: Victor Lyle killed Eleanor Whitcombe because the imminent exposure of his embezzlement scheme.
solution.method: Eleanor Whitcombe was fatally stabbed with the knife.
solution.timeOfDeath: After 8:35 pm and before discovery at 8:45 pm
solution.opportunity: Victor Lyle claimed to have left at 8:30 pm. The timeline evidence places the attack after Gareth Pembroke and Clara Haversham left together at 8:35 pm; the teal-and-cream tweed fibres on the knife and the black and gold fragment recovered from the glass place Victor Lyle inside afterwards. Victor Lyle therefore had the only evidenced opportunity to be alone with Eleanor Whitcombe and reach the knife during the murder window.
